faqs.q: Is trauma dumping a real diagnosis?
faqs.a: No. Trauma dumping is a popular phrase that spread on social media, not a clinical diagnosis. It describes a one-sided pattern of unloading distressing details on someone without warning or consent, rather than a recognized mental health condition.

faqs.q: What is the difference between trauma dumping and venting?
faqs.a: Venting is usually shorter, mutual, and offered to someone who has agreed to listen. Trauma dumping tends to be a lot at once, arrives without a heads-up, and leaves little room for the other person to opt in or step back.

faqs.q: How do I respond if someone trauma dumps on me?
faqs.a: It is fine to set a kind limit, such as saying you care but cannot hold this right now, and to suggest they reach out to a therapist or crisis line. Setting that boundary protects the relationship rather than ending support.
